phenomenon: Why learning cards become longer and lower-signal when every possible section stays permanently visible.
causal_chain: A wide template invites filler, filler normalizes section completion, section completion hides weak signal, and the card gradually optimizes for fullness instead of retrieval value.
key_prerequisites: The template must make it easy to keep optional sections visible even when their content is weak.
weakest_step: The weakest step is review discipline; without active pruning, optional sections become permanent ballast.
alternative_explanations: Some bloat comes from unclear source threads rather than from template width alone.
scope: This mechanism mainly explains mature card families with many reusable sections, not tiny scratch notes.
failure_boundaries: If the card is too new to have real optional sections yet, bloat drift is not the main cause of verbosity.
supporting_evidence: Across filled samples, progression and review scaffolding consume a large share of rendered words even when the card's real learning payload is already complete.
counter_cases: A short template can still produce bloated cards when the thread itself is badly scoped.
compressed_explanation: Section bloat drift happens when template completion becomes more important than signal density.
validation_question: Is the extra section carrying new retrieval value, or only proving that the template was filled?

upstream_concepts: [[Single-Card Boundary]] | Weak boundaries make it easier for extra sections to sneak in.
trigger_conditions: [[Always-On Review Scaffolding]] | Permanent checklists and status blocks trigger the drift.
downstream_results: [[Low-Signal Cards]] | The mechanism lowers retrieval density and raises maintenance burden.
adjacent_mechanisms: [[Scope Drift]] | Scope drift widens the topic, while section bloat drift widens the template.

prerequisites: [[Stable Card Family]] | The mechanism appears only after the template gains enough reusable structure.
enables: [[Section Priority Compression]] | Understanding the mechanism justifies trimming action.
contrasts: [[Rich Evidence Density]] | High detail is not the same as low-signal bloat.
corrections: [[More Sections Make a Better Learning Card]] | Correcting the misconception removes one cause of this drift.

direct_routes: When the problem is deciding what to cut first, route to [[Section Priority Compression]].
If the problem is not template width but topic width, return to [[Single-Card Boundary]].
secondary_routes: If [[Section Priority Compression]] trims the card but the urge to re-expand remains, continue to [[More Sections Make a Better Learning Card]].
gap_signals: If the graph explains bloat but not how to measure retrieval density, add a new Concept card for signal density.
stop_rules: Stop using this mechanism after one full trim pass if the card is still weak because the topic itself was misrouted.

current_status_notes: The mechanism already explains why mature card templates tend to overproduce low-value sections.
next_goal: Add a stronger distinction between topic drift and template drift.
stable_checklist: Document one repeated sign of template-caused bloat.
expert_ready_checklist: Add stronger diagnosis routes between scope drift and section bloat drift.

main_reasons: The mechanism already clarifies a repeatable cause of card verbosity.
missing_evidence: It still needs a stronger switch rule for distinguishing section drift from topic drift.
next_rules: If trimming section count does not improve retrieval, inspect topic routing before pruning further.

current_upgrade_tasks: Capture one card where trimming the template did not solve the underlying problem.
